title: Pow(x, n)
constraints: Either x is not 0 or n > 0
The answer fits in a double
followUps: Write the naive O(n) loop first, then a recursive O(log n) version using pow(x, n) = pow(x, n/2) * pow(x, n/2).
Carefully handle negative n: pow(x, -n) = 1 / pow(x, n). Beware the edge case n = -2^31.
Can you write an iterative O(log n) version using the binary representation of n?
